key_insight: HVAF spraying can produce thick NiTi coatings that retain martensitic transformation and shape-memory functionality, but only after post-spray annealing.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical route to durable, functional NiTi surface coatings for components needing wear resistance plus thermal actuation or recovery behavior.
method: Characterized composition, porosity, microstructure, phase transformation, and thermomechanical response
evidence_signal: The coatings were evaluated by microstructural/phase analyses and by functional tests including 3-point bending, nanoindentation, and scratch testing, showing martensitic transformation and actuation after annealing.
watch_item: As-sprayed coatings were inhomogeneous and defect-rich, so post-deposition annealing is necessary and tensile strength remains limited.
paper_title: HVAF Spraying of NiTi Coatings: Microstructure, Phase Transformation and Shape Memory Behavior
